title: Entity 140
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Entity 140: "Flesh Bacteria"}}__NOTOC__ {{Entity Class|Agg=0|Int=1|Dan=5|no=140|Hab=Level 563}}
''Do not trust your hunger inside the Banquet Hall. After all, it is not yours anymore.'' ''— M.E.G. Containment Advisory, Level 563''
{{Blurimage|file=A microscopic picture taken in a M.E.G lab of The Flesh Bacteria.jpg|align=left|caption=A microscopic picture taken in a M.E.G lab of The Flesh Bacteria}} ==Description== '''Entity 140''', commonly referred to as the '''Flesh Bacteria''', is a pervasive microscopic biological organism endemic to '''[[Level 563|Level 563.]]''' Unlike conventional pathogens, it does not appear to exist as a single localized infection, but rather as a distributed system present throughout the Banquet Hall’s food supply, organic residue, and structural composition. Contamination is most commonly associated with consumable materials found within the level, though prolonged exposure to the environment itself has also been linked to infection onset. Once introduced into a host, '''Entity 140''' initiates gradual physiological and cognitive alterations centered around hunger response, sensory perception, and food-related fixation. ==Behavior== '''Entity 140''' spreads primarily through ingestion of contaminated food or direct contact with infected organic material. Upon entry into a host, it begins altering neural pathways associated with hunger, reward, and sensory interpretation. Early-stage infection is typically characterized by mild disorientation and increased attention toward food sources within '''[[Level 563|Level 563.]]''' As infection progresses, affected individuals exhibit increasingly compulsive consumption behavior, reduced aversion to unsafe materials, and a marked preference for items originating from '''[[Level 563|the Banquet Hall.]]''' In advanced cases, subjects may lose the ability to distinguish contaminated material from safe provisions. There is evidence to suggest that '''Entity 140''' also influences environmental perception, causing infected individuals to describe foul odors and decayed material as pleasant or desirable. This effect appears to strengthen with prolonged exposure.{{Blurimage|file=Brainscan.jpg|alt=A video of a wanderer taking a video before colliding with a '''Filing Cabinet'''|caption=A brain scan taken of a wanderer affected by Entity 140.|align=left}} ==Discovery == '''Entity 140''' was first formally documented following a series of unexplained behavioral incidents reported by M.E.G. reconnaissance teams operating within [[Level 563|'''Level 563.''']] Initial accounts described wanderers becoming unusually fixated on banquet food sources, followed by rapid cognitive decline and disappearance from observed areas. Subsequent recovery missions retrieved multiple contaminated samples from dining tables and kitchen-adjacent spaces. Laboratory analysis confirmed the presence of an unknown biological agent embedded within both consumable material and surrounding organic structures. Further investigation revealed a consistent correlation between ingestion of Level 563 food items and onset of infection symptoms, leading to the classification of '''Entity 140''' as a level-wide biological hazard rather than a localized organism.{{Blurimage|file=Entity 140.png|caption=More microscopic specimens of Entity 140.|align=left}} ==Appearance / Biology== '''Entity 140''' is not visible in its primary form without advanced magnification. Microscopic analysis reveals dense, irregular clusters of rapidly dividing cells exhibiting both bacterial and tissue-like properties. These structures demonstrate an unusual ability to integrate with existing organic matter, effectively blurring the line between pathogen and biological material. In high-concentration environments, '''Entity 140''' contributes to macroscopic formations resembling living tissue, including the '''Organic Substructure''' found within walls, flooring, and decorative architecture. These formations may exhibit slow pulsing, temperature variation, and reactive movement when disturbed. No known terminal state of the organism has been observed, as samples continue to remain active under standard containment conditions. == Stages of Infection == |-|Stage 1 — Initial Exposure= The earliest stage begins shortly after ingestion of contaminated material. Symptoms are subtle and often dismissed as fatigue or stress. Wanderers report a slight increase in appetite, even if recently fed, along with a faint sensory “warmth” associated with food found within Level 563. At this stage, individuals are still fully aware of their surroundings and can function normally, though mild fixation on dining areas may begin. |-|Stage 2 — Sensory Alteration= As the infection progresses, perception begins to shift. Smells within Level 563—particularly meat, baked goods, and cooked dishes—become noticeably more appealing. Previously unpleasant odors may become neutral or even desirable. Subjects often begin returning to banquet halls repeatedly without clear reason. Cognitive focus narrows toward food sources, and external supplies may be ignored in favor of in-level provisions. |-|Stage 3 — Behavioral Compulsion= At this stage, infection significantly impacts decision-making. Wanderers experience strong, intrusive urges to consume any food found within Level 563, regardless of caution or prior knowledge of contamination risks. Social behavior may deteriorate, and individuals often isolate themselves near banquet tables or kitchen-adjacent areas. Reports frequently describe affected persons accepting food from unknown entities or unexplained sources. |-|Stage 4 — Assimilation Onset= Physical and biological changes begin to manifest. Subjects may exhibit abnormal tissue growth, increased body temperature, and subtle changes in skin texture resembling organic material found within Level 563. Cognitive function declines sharply. Victims often lose the ability to distinguish between safe and unsafe substances, and may actively seek out contaminated food sources. In some cases, individuals begin to show structural integration with nearby organic surfaces. |-|Stage 5 — Full Integration= The final stage represents complete assimilation. The subject is no longer distinguishable as a normal human entity. Physical remains are either fully absorbed into the Organic Substructure or transformed into stationary biological formations resembling banquet decor or surrounding architecture. No confirmed recovery cases have been documented beyond this stage. Entities at this point are presumed to be permanently integrated into Level 563’s biological system. ==Do's == * Use only sealed, externally sourced food and water supplies while inside '''[[Level 563]]''' * Maintain distance from exposed organic wall material and suspected infection zones * Exit the level immediately upon experiencing abnormal hunger, sensory distortion, or behavioral urges * Report any observed structural “breathing” or pulsing to '''[[M.E.G.]]''' personnel if possible ==Dont's== * Do not consume any food, drink, or edible material found within [[Level 563|'''Level 563''']] * Do not make physical contact with the Organic Substructure or flesh-like architectural growths * Do not remain seated or stationary at banquet tables for extended periods * Do not trust individuals displaying abnormal fixation on food or repeated invitations to eat {{Collapse|initial=|final=|body= }} [[Category:Entities]] [[Category:Mimic Entities]]
